summary: Full backend + frontend e2e regression of MycoTrack PostgreSQL refactor. 20/20 pytest cases pass (health, JWT cookie auth, RBAC for admin/operator/viewer across all routers, species/locations/recipes CRUD, cultures create/update/lineage + status validation + 405 on DELETE, culture-events admin-only delete, soft-delete users, cannot deactivate self, register defaults to viewer). 16/16 Playwright UI checks pass (admin login, sidebar nav, species + culture creation via modals, events drawer add, lineage modal, logout, viewer hides Users & Roles nav and write buttons).
critical_code_review_comments: auth/refresh: refresh endpoint does not include email in payload from refresh token (refresh tokens are minted without email claim), so newly issued access tokens have email='' — functional impact minimal because email is not used in auth flow, but inconsistent. Consider re-fetching user from DB during refresh to also enforce is_active=true (currently a deactivated user with a still-valid refresh token can mint a new access token; deps.get_current_user does reject inactive users on each request, so safe in practice).
auth_testing.md mentions brute force lockout after 5 fails; no lockout is implemented (each wrong password just returns 401 without rate limit). Not in spec for this iteration but worth noting.
DELETE /api/cultures/{id} is correctly absent → returns 405 by design. Confirm this is the intended UX (no soft-delete via status='discarded' enforcement at API level).
FastAPI on_event('startup') is deprecated; consider lifespan handlers when upgrading.
test_credentials: admin@mycotrack.io / MycoAdmin#2026, operator@mycotrack.io / Operator#2026, viewer@mycotrack.io / Viewer#2026 — all working
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_-prefixed species/locations/recipes/cultures/events/users via the API; cultures cannot be hard-deleted by design so leftover TEST_ cultures remain (acceptable, code uses unique uuid tags).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest entrypoint: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_mycotrack_api.py -v. BASE_URL is taken from REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL env var. Auth uses httpOnly cookies — requests.Session() carries them automatically. Cultures have no DELETE endpoint by design (DELETE /api/cultures/{id} returns 405). Culture-event DELETE is admin-only (403 for operator/viewer). User DELETE is soft delete (is_active=false). All three seed users (admin/operator/viewer) exist in Postgres.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. Only one initial test failure was a test-data issue (case-sensitive email comparison) — backend correctly lowercases emails on register/login; test was updated to compare against the lowercased value.
